text.source_text: अथ यदुक्तं वाक्यशेषगताज्जीवलिङ्गान्मुख्यप्राणलिङ्गाच्च तयोरेवान्यतरस्येह ग्रहणं न्याय्यं न परमेश्वरस्येति , तत्परिहर्तव्यम् । अत्रोच्यते — परिहृतं चैतत् ‘ नोपासात्रैविध्यादाश्रितत्वादिह तद्योगात् ’ (ब्र. सू. १ । १ । ३१) इत्यत्र । त्रिविधं ह्यत्रोपासनमेवं सति प्रसज्येत — जीवोपासनं मुख्यप्राणोपासनं ब्रह्मोपासनं चेति । न चैतन्न्याय्यम् । उपक्रमोपसंहाराभ्यां हि ब्रह्मविषयत्वमस्य वाक्यस्यावगम्यते । तत्रोपक्रमस्य तावद्ब्रह्मविषयत्वं दर्शितम् । उपसंहारस्यापि निरतिशयफलश्रवणाद्ब्रह्मविषयत्वं दृश्यते — ‘ सर्वान्पाप्मनोऽपहत्य सर्वेषां च भूतानां श्रैष्ठ्यं स्वाराज्यमाधिपत्यं पर्येति य एवं वेद ’ इति । नन्वेवं सति प्रतर्दनवाक्यनिर्णयेनैवेदमपि वाक्यं निर्णीयेत । न निर्णीयते , ‘ यस्य वैतत्कर्म ’ इत्यस्य ब्रह्मविषयत्वेन तत्र अनिर्धारितत्वात् । तस्मादत्र जीवमुख्यप्राणशङ्का पुनरुत्पद्यमाना निवर्त्यते । प्राणशब्दोऽपि ब्रह्मविषयो दृष्टः ‘ प्राणबन्धनं हि सोम्य मनः ’ (छा. उ. ६ । ८ । २) इत्यत्र । जीवलिङ्गमप्युपक्रमोपसंहारयोर्ब्रह्मविषयत्वादभेदाभिप्रायेण योजयितव्यम् ॥ १७ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: atha yaduktaṃ vākyaśeṣagatājjīvaliṅgānmukhyaprāṇaliṅgācca tayorevānyatarasyeha grahaṇaṃ nyāyyaṃ na parameśvarasyeti , tatparihartavyam | atrocyate — parihṛtaṃ caitat ‘ nopāsātraividhyādāśritatvādiha tadyogāt ’ (bra. sū. 1 | 1 | 31) ityatra | trividhaṃ hyatropāsanamevaṃ sati prasajyeta — jīvopāsanaṃ mukhyaprāṇopāsanaṃ brahmopāsanaṃ ceti | na caitannyāyyam | upakramopasaṃhārābhyāṃ hi brahmaviṣayatvamasya vākyasyāvagamyate | tatropakramasya tāvadbrahmaviṣayatvaṃ darśitam | upasaṃhārasyāpi niratiśayaphalaśravaṇādbrahmaviṣayatvaṃ dṛśyate — ‘ sarvānpāpmano'pahatya sarveṣāṃ ca bhūtānāṃ śraiṣṭhyaṃ svārājyamādhipatyaṃ paryeti ya evaṃ veda ’ iti | nanvevaṃ sati pratardanavākyanirṇayenaivedamapi vākyaṃ nirṇīyeta | na nirṇīyate , ‘ yasya vaitatkarma ’ ityasya brahmaviṣayatvena tatra anirdhāritatvāt | tasmādatra jīvamukhyaprāṇaśaṅkā punarutpadyamānā nivartyate | prāṇaśabdo'pi brahmaviṣayo dṛṣṭaḥ ‘ prāṇabandhanaṃ hi somya manaḥ ’ (chā. u. 6 | 8 | 2) ityatra | jīvaliṅgamapyupakramopasaṃhārayorbrahmaviṣayatvādabhedābhiprāyeṇa yojayitavyam || 17 ||

translation.literal_translation: As for the claim that, because of indicators in the sentence remainder, one should accept either jiva or chief prana here and not the Lord, that must be answered. The answer is: this has already been addressed in the earlier discussion of tri-fold upasana. If one insisted on separate jiva-, prana-, and Brahman-upasanas here, the passage would become improperly tripartite. But opening and conclusion show Brahman as the subject, and the closing fruit is unsurpassed ('having destroyed all sin... attaining lordship'). Therefore the renewed jiva/prana doubt is removed. Even 'prana' can denote Brahman in other shruti contexts, and apparent jiva-markers are to be integrated under non-difference intent when opening and conclusion are Brahman-centered.
translation.technical_translation: This block neutralizes linga-fragmentation by invoking prakarana unity and phala-criterion. The possibility of three independent objects of meditation is rejected as structurally incoherent. Upakrama-upasamhara plus niratishaya-phala force Brahman-reference. Consequently, prana- and jiva-lingas are reinterpreted as subordinate or abheda-oriented indicators rather than primary topic-setters.
translation.interpretive_note: Hermeneutic hierarchy: contextual unity outranks isolated linga markers.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: This draft compresses cross-reference to earlier sutra discussion for readability.
Final pass may restore more explicit Sanskrit technical terms for each hermeneutic move.
